tests.name: Search Engine Discovery & Reconnaissance
tests.description: Use search engines to find exposed information, internal paths, credentials, or sensitive documents indexed publicly.

tests.name: Fingerprint Web Server
tests.description: Identify the web server type, version, and underlying OS via banners, headers, and error responses.

tests.name: Review Webserver Metafiles for Information Leakage
tests.description: Examine robots.txt, sitemap.xml, and other metafiles for sensitive path disclosures.

tests.name: Enumerate Applications on Webserver
tests.description: Identify all web applications hosted on the same server via port scanning and virtual host enumeration.

tests.name: Review Webpage Content for Information Leakage
tests.description: Inspect HTML comments, hidden fields, JavaScript files, and source code for sensitive information.

tests.name: Identify Application Entry Points
tests.description: Map all user-controlled input vectors: forms, query parameters, headers, cookies, API endpoints.

tests.name: Map Execution Paths Through Application
tests.description: Trace application workflows and identify all code paths that could process attacker-controlled input.

tests.name: Fingerprint Web Application Framework
tests.description: Identify the framework and version (Django, Rails, Laravel, etc.) via headers, cookies, and error pages.

tests.name: Fingerprint Web Application
tests.description: Identify the CMS or application type (WordPress, Drupal, etc.) and enumerate installed plugins/themes.

tests.name: Map Application Architecture
tests.description: Identify load balancers, WAFs, CDNs, reverse proxies, and backend services in the application stack.

tests.name: Test Network Infrastructure Configuration
tests.description: Review network components for misconfigurations: open ports, unpatched services, weak cipher suites.

tests.name: Test Application Platform Configuration
tests.description: Check web server and application server config for default settings, unnecessary modules, debug modes.

tests.name: Test File Extension Handling
tests.description: Verify how the server handles unexpected file extensions — check for source code disclosure or execution.

tests.name: Review Backup & Unreferenced Files
tests.description: Discover backup files (.bak, .old, ~), unreferenced pages, and forgotten test files that leak source or config.

tests.name: Enumerate Admin Interfaces
tests.description: Find admin panels, management consoles, and privileged endpoints exposed on the server.

tests.name: Test HTTP Methods
tests.description: Enumerate enabled HTTP methods (PUT, DELETE, TRACE) and test for dangerous method abuse.

tests.name: Test HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS)
tests.description: Verify HSTS header is present, has appropriate max-age, and includes subdomains where applicable.

tests.name: Test RIA Cross Domain Policy
tests.description: Review crossdomain.xml and clientaccesspolicy.xml for overly permissive cross-domain access.

tests.name: Test File Permissions
tests.description: Verify server files and directories have appropriate permissions; check for world-writable paths.

tests.name: Test for Subdomain Takeover
tests.description: Identify dangling DNS entries pointing to deprovisioned cloud/SaaS resources susceptible to takeover.

tests.name: Test Cloud Storage
tests.description: Check S3 buckets, Azure blobs, and GCS buckets for public read/write access and sensitive data exposure.

tests.name: Test Content Security Policy (CSP)
tests.description: Evaluate the CSP header for overly permissive directives, unsafe-inline, unsafe-eval, or missing directives.

tests.name: Test Role Definitions
tests.description: Verify roles are clearly defined and enforced; check for role confusion or privilege overlap.

tests.name: Test User Registration Process
tests.description: Test registration for account enumeration, weak validation, and ability to register with privileged identifiers.

tests.name: Test Account Provisioning Process
tests.description: Verify provisioning workflows require proper authorization and cannot be abused to escalate privileges.

tests.name: Test for Account Enumeration
tests.description: Determine if the application leaks valid usernames via login errors, registration, or password reset responses.

tests.name: Test for Weak Username Policy
tests.description: Check if usernames are guessable (sequential IDs, email addresses) and if the policy enforces uniqueness.

tests.name: Test Credentials Over Encrypted Channel
tests.description: Confirm all authentication occurs over HTTPS; check for mixed content, HTTP fallback, or credential leakage.

tests.name: Test Default Credentials
tests.description: Attempt login with vendor-supplied default credentials on all services and admin interfaces.

tests.name: Test Account Lockout Mechanism
tests.description: Test brute-force protections: lockout thresholds, reset behaviour, IP-based versus account-based lockout.

tests.name: Test for Authentication Bypass
tests.description: Attempt to bypass authentication via parameter manipulation, forced browsing, or request forgery.

tests.name: Test Remember Password Functionality
tests.description: Inspect remember-me tokens for weak entropy, insecure storage, or indefinite validity.

tests.name: Test Browser Cache Weaknesses
tests.description: Verify sensitive pages set Cache-Control: no-store and are not retrievable from browser back button.

tests.name: Test Weak Password Policy
tests.description: Review minimum length, complexity requirements, password reuse policy, and common password rejection.

tests.name: Test Security Questions
tests.description: Evaluate secret question/answer mechanisms for guessability, enumeration, and bypass potential.

tests.name: Test Password Reset Functionality
tests.description: Test reset flows for token predictability, reuse, expiry, account enumeration, and host header injection.

tests.name: Test Alternative Authentication Channels
tests.description: Identify weaker authentication paths (mobile app, API, legacy portal) that bypass primary controls.

tests.name: Test Directory Traversal / File Include
tests.description: Test for path traversal in file parameters; attempt to read arbitrary files or include remote content.

tests.name: Test for Authorization Schema Bypass
tests.description: Attempt to access privileged functions or resources by manipulating roles, parameters, or HTTP methods.

tests.name: Test for Privilege Escalation
tests.description: Attempt horizontal and vertical privilege escalation by modifying user identifiers or role parameters.

tests.name: Test for Insecure Direct Object References (IDOR)
tests.description: Access objects (records, files, accounts) belonging to other users by manipulating object identifiers.

tests.name: Test Session Management Schema
tests.description: Analyse session token entropy, predictability, and uniqueness; verify tokens are regenerated after login.

tests.name: Test Cookie Attributes
tests.description: Verify session cookies have Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite attributes and appropriate scope/expiry.

tests.name: Test for Session Fixation
tests.description: Determine if the application accepts attacker-supplied session tokens or fails to rotate them on login.

tests.name: Test for Exposed Session Variables
tests.description: Check if session tokens appear in URLs, Referer headers, logs, or are otherwise improperly exposed.

tests.name: Test for CSRF
tests.description: Verify CSRF tokens are present, unique per session, and validated for all state-changing requests.

tests.name: Test Logout Functionality
tests.description: Confirm logout invalidates the server-side session and that tokens cannot be reused post-logout.

tests.name: Test Session Timeout
tests.description: Verify idle and absolute session timeouts are enforced server-side and sessions expire appropriately.

tests.name: Test for Session Puzzling
tests.description: Check if session variables are used in multiple contexts allowing session variable overloading attacks.

tests.name: Test for Session Hijacking
tests.description: Assess susceptibility to session hijacking via network sniffing, XSS-based token theft, or token prediction.

tests.name: Test for Reflected XSS
tests.description: Inject script payloads into all reflected input vectors; verify output encoding and CSP effectiveness.

tests.name: Test for Stored XSS
tests.description: Submit persistent script payloads via all stored input fields; verify they are not rendered unescaped.

tests.name: Test for HTTP Verb Tampering
tests.description: Test for authentication/authorisation bypass by switching HTTP methods (GET vs POST vs HEAD).

tests.name: Test for HTTP Parameter Pollution
tests.description: Submit duplicate parameters to confuse server-side parsing and bypass input validation or WAF rules.

tests.name: Test for SQL Injection
tests.description: Test all database-backed inputs for error-based, blind, time-based, and out-of-band SQL injection.

tests.name: Test for LDAP Injection
tests.description: Inject LDAP metacharacters into authentication and search inputs to bypass controls or enumerate directory.

tests.name: Test for XML Injection
tests.description: Test XML inputs for injection, XXE (external entity), and XPath injection vulnerabilities.

tests.name: Test for SSI Injection
tests.description: Check for Server-Side Include injection in inputs that are embedded in server-parsed HTML files.

tests.name: Test for XPath Injection
tests.description: Inject XPath syntax into inputs used to query XML data stores; attempt authentication bypass or data extraction.

tests.name: Test for IMAP/SMTP Injection
tests.description: Inject mail headers and commands via webmail interfaces to relay spam, access mailboxes, or enumerate users.

tests.name: Test for Code Injection
tests.description: Test inputs used in dynamic code execution (eval, exec) for arbitrary code injection.

tests.name: Test for Command Injection
tests.description: Inject OS commands via inputs passed to shell functions; test for blind injection via time delays or OOB.

tests.name: Test for Format String Injection
tests.description: Test inputs used in printf-style format functions for format string vulnerabilities.

tests.name: Test for Incubated Vulnerability
tests.description: Test for vulnerabilities that are triggered asynchronously or by a different user/process than the injector.

tests.name: Test for HTTP Splitting/Smuggling
tests.description: Inject CRLF sequences into HTTP headers; test for request smuggling between front-end and back-end servers.

tests.name: Test for HTTP Incoming Requests
tests.description: Monitor and analyse all incoming HTTP requests for unexpected parameters, headers, or malformed input.

tests.name: Test for Host Header Injection
tests.description: Inject arbitrary Host headers to trigger password reset poisoning, cache poisoning, or SSRF.

tests.name: Test for Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI)
tests.description: Inject template syntax into inputs rendered by template engines (Jinja2, Twig, Freemarker) to achieve RCE.

tests.name: Test for Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
tests.description: Test inputs that trigger server-side HTTP requests for SSRF to internal services or cloud metadata endpoints.

tests.name: Test for Improper Error Handling
tests.description: Trigger application errors and verify responses don't leak stack traces, internal paths, or DB structure.

tests.name: Test for Stack Traces
tests.description: Confirm production error pages show generic messages and that verbose errors are only in development mode.

tests.name: Test Weak Transport Layer Security
tests.description: Scan for weak TLS versions (SSLv3, TLS 1.0/1.1), weak cipher suites, expired certs, and missing features.

tests.name: Test for Padding Oracle
tests.description: Test CBC-mode encrypted tokens for padding oracle vulnerabilities enabling decryption or forgery.

tests.name: Test for Sensitive Data Over Unencrypted Channel
tests.description: Verify no sensitive data (credentials, PII, tokens) is transmitted over unencrypted HTTP connections.

tests.name: Test for Weak Encryption
tests.description: Identify use of weak or broken algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES, ECB mode) for sensitive data storage or transit.

tests.name: Test Business Logic Data Validation
tests.description: Verify the application enforces business rules on all input, not just UI-level controls.

tests.name: Test Ability to Forge Requests
tests.description: Replay or modify legitimate requests to perform unintended actions (e.g., negative prices, skipped steps).

tests.name: Test Integrity Checks
tests.description: Verify the application detects and rejects tampered data; check signatures, checksums, and MAC validation.

tests.name: Test for Process Timing
tests.description: Test for race conditions and time-of-check/time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in critical operations.

tests.name: Test Function Use Limits
tests.description: Verify rate limits and usage caps are enforced server-side for sensitive functions (OTP, voucher codes).

tests.name: Test for Workflow Circumvention
tests.description: Attempt to skip required workflow steps (payment, verification) by directly accessing later-stage URLs.

tests.name: Test Defences Against Application Misuse
tests.description: Attempt to abuse legitimate features in unintended ways; verify anomaly detection or rate limiting exists.

tests.name: Test Upload of Unexpected File Types
tests.description: Upload non-whitelisted file types to upload functions; check for client-side-only MIME type validation.

tests.name: Test Upload of Malicious Files
tests.description: Upload web shells, polyglots, and malicious files; check if uploaded files are executed by the server.

tests.name: Test for DOM-Based XSS
tests.description: Identify sinks that write attacker-controlled data to the DOM (innerHTML, document.write, eval) without sanitisation.

tests.name: Test for JavaScript Execution
tests.description: Test inputs that flow into JavaScript execution contexts for injection via prototype pollution or eval sinks.

tests.name: Test for HTML Injection
tests.description: Inject HTML markup into inputs reflected in responses; test for phishing and UI redressing potential.

tests.name: Test for Client-Side URL Redirect
tests.description: Test open redirect vulnerabilities in client-side redirect parameters for phishing and token theft.

tests.name: Test for CSS Injection
tests.description: Inject CSS into inputs rendered in style contexts; check for data exfiltration via CSS selectors.

tests.name: Test for Client-Side Resource Manipulation
tests.description: Test inputs controlling resource URLs (scripts, stylesheets) for manipulation to load attacker content.

tests.name: Test Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)
tests.description: Verify CORS policy does not allow arbitrary origins to read sensitive responses; check ACAO: * on credentialed requests.

tests.name: Test for Clickjacking
tests.description: Verify X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors prevents the application from being embedded in iframes.

tests.name: Test WebSockets
tests.description: Test WebSocket connections for lack of authentication, missing origin validation, and injection vulnerabilities.

tests.name: Test Web Messaging
tests.description: Review postMessage handlers for missing origin validation allowing cross-origin message injection.

tests.name: Test Browser Storage
tests.description: Review localStorage/sessionStorage/IndexedDB for sensitive data stored client-side without encryption.

tests.name: Test for Cross-Site Script Inclusion (XSSI)
tests.description: Test JSON endpoints for data leakage when included as scripts in attacker-controlled pages.

tests.name: Test GraphQL
tests.description: Test GraphQL endpoints for introspection exposure, query depth attacks, batching abuse, and injection.

tests.name: Test REST
tests.description: Test REST APIs for broken object-level auth, mass assignment, improper HTTP method handling, and injection.

tests.name: Test SOAP/XML
tests.description: Test SOAP services for XXE, WSDL enumeration, parameter tampering, and authentication weaknesses.
